1.description: (Color online) Experimental Implementation of Asset Supply vs. Real-World Supply Schedule Notes. For both panels, the left vertical axis corresponds to aggregate asset supply, whereas the right vertical axis corresponds to asset influx. The term block subsidy in the right panel is commonly used to highlight that new coins are introduced as a reward to successful miners. The left panel shows the evolution of asset supply in our experiment under the assumption that all subjects mine at full capacity in all periods.

2.description: Theoretical CAR Across Trading Periods Note. Assuming mining at full capacity in each period.

3.description: Trading Prices, Volume, and Cash-to-Asset Ratio in Study 1 Treatments Notes. (a) Median of volume-weighted price per period. (b) Session-median trading volume per period. (c) Realized session-median CAR.

4.description: Median Volume-Weighted Price and Mining Cost per Period in Mining Treatments

5.description: Order Book Analysis Notes. (a) Origin of accepted trades. (b) Asks. (c) Bids. In panel (a) we report the median ratio of origin of accepted trades per period per treatment. Each node corresponds to the proportion of completed trades that initiated from either bids or asks. In panels (b) and (c) we report asks and bids per treatment. We calculate the volume-weighted average per period for each session, and report the median value of the nine sessions in each period per treatment.

6.description: Trader Average Action by Role Notes. (a) Mining-Half and (b) Gift-Half. Histograms present the distribution of trader average action across all periods.

7.description: Transfers from Miners to Non-miners in Half Treatments Notes. Median proportion of transfers from miners to non-miners in Mining-Half (and “miners” to “non-miners” in Gift-Half) relative to the outstanding assets in the market per period. This proportion can range from −1, if all assets are transferred from non-miners to miners, to 1, if all assets are transferred from miners to non-miners.

8.description: Timing of Trades in Mining Treatments Notes. Each line depicts the evolution of assets within a given session over time. In most cases, we observe a steep increase in assets (i.e., mining) at the beginning of each period, and a rather flat trajectory till the end of a given period.

9.description: Median of Volume-Weighted Price per Period in Airdrop Treatments
